description: A candid color event photograph (no on-image text or graphic overlay) of the naturalization ceremony at Dodger Stadium, shot from the field looking up at the crowd. Hundreds of seated and standing attendees fill the stadium seating, many with right hands raised taking the oath of allegiance. In the front row stands a line of people in U.S. military dress uniforms (Army, Marine Corps, Air Force, etc.) alongside civilians, with empty white folding chairs and small American flags visible. 'WELCOME TO DODGER STADIUM' signage rings the stands in the background. This is a documentary photo of a large official ceremony, not a designed meme or poster. It accompanies the celebratory tweet about 2,100 new U.S. citizens from 120 countries. Intent is positive PR around citizenship/naturalization and immigrant military service; no slogan or designed typography appears on the image. From Aug 2022 (Biden-era).
notable_text: (none on image; incidental venue signage 'WELCOME TO DODGER STADIUM'. Tweet celebrates 2,100 new citizens from 120 countries)
